subject: Accounting
title: Inventory Turnover
description: A fast breakdown of Inventory Turnover for builders.

hook.text: A store has $1 million in inventory. If it takes a year to sell, that's a million dollars stuck.

buildup.text: Inventory turnover measures how many times a company sells and replaces its stock per year.

discovery.text: Higher turnover means products move fast. Lower turnover means goods are sitting on shelves.

twist.text: Too-high turnover can mean stockouts—lost sales because items aren't available.

climax.text: Finding the right balance between too much inventory and too little is a constant challenge.

punchline.text: Inventory that doesn't move is cash that doesn't flow.

quiz.question: What does high inventory turnover indicate?
options: The company has too much stock
